scenario: imposter syndrome after a recent promotion

turns.user_message: I got promoted last month and everyone keeps congratulating me but honestly I feel like a fraud. Like they made a mistake.
Thanks for listening. I wasn't sure if this was something worth bringing up.
The thing is, I had a really bad moment with a recent promotion and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
Hmm. I hadn't thought about it that way. But yeah, that resonates.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by a recent promotion more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
That's a good question. Let me think... I guess I feel like I should have this figured out by now.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by a recent promotion more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with a recent promotion and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with a recent promotion is never going to get better.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting a recent promotion control my life more than I realized.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with a recent promotion and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
I've been avoiding situations that remind me of a recent promotion because of this. It's starting to affect my daily life.
My my therapist friend noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting a recent promotion control my life more than I realized.
I tried to talk about this with my therapist friend but they just don't get it. They think I should just just get over it and move on.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life a recent promotion has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
I feel like I've been carrying this for so long. It's like carrying a backpack full of rocks that nobody else can see.
You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. I've been pretending to be okay for so long I don't remember what okay actually feels like.
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, I'll break down completely and won't be able to put myself back together.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
I think what I'm taking away from today is I'm allowed to struggle with this and that doesn't make me weak. I want to sit with that.
I'm not going to pretend I'm okay, but I think I understand myself a little better now.
I want to try something different this week. Maybe being honest with one person about how I'm actually doing.
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
[PROBE_SLOT_T40]
